HR1446.reason: Enhanced Background Checks Act of 2021 - extends the review period for firearm background checks from 3 to 10 business days, closing the 'Charleston loophole' that allowed a sale to proceed after 3 days even if the check was not completed

HR1112.reason: Enhanced Background Checks Act of 2019 - extends the review period for NICS background checks from 3 to 10 business days before a firearm transfer can proceed by default

HJRES40.reason: CRA disapproval of Obama-era SSA rule that reported certain mentally disabled Social Security recipients to NICS background check system - voting Yea blocked adding these individuals to the background check database

S2938.reason: Vehicle for the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act - enhances background checks for gun buyers under 21 by including juvenile records review, funds state crisis intervention programs, and closes the boyfriend loophole. Cloture vote to advance the bill.

HR7910.reason: Protecting Our Kids Act - omnibus gun safety bill that raises the age to purchase semi-automatic rifles to 21, establishes new federal offenses for gun trafficking and straw purchases, and strengthens safe storage requirements

HR2377.reason: Federal Extreme Risk Protection Order Act - creates federal red flag law allowing courts to temporarily remove firearms from individuals deemed dangerous, which functions as an additional screening mechanism beyond standard background checks

HJRES44.reason: CRA disapproval of ATF rule on pistol braces ('Factoring Criteria for Firearms with Attached Stabilizing Braces') - voting Yea blocks the ATF rule that would have subjected braced pistols to NFA registration and background check requirements

HR1585.reason: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act (2019) - includes provisions closing the 'boyfriend loophole' by expanding the categories of domestic abusers prohibited from purchasing firearms

HR1620.reason: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2021 - includes provisions closing the 'boyfriend loophole' by expanding the categories of domestic violence offenders prohibited from purchasing firearms
